title: Polylogue
summary: A sleek web-based multi-agent discussion interface built around the idea of many minds in one conversation.
description: Polylogue is one of Phil's most recent public repos and a strong example of interface-focused AI experimentation.
The concept centers on structured multi-agent conversation, pushing beyond single-chat patterns toward richer collaborative reasoning surfaces.

media.alt: A reconstructed screenshot based on the Polylogue interface design.
media.caption: A dark, multi-agent discussion interface rendered from the project's public template.

media.alt: A highland observatory city representing Polylogue.
media.caption: A science city for multi-agent interfaces and collaborative reasoning.

greatWorks.title: A Thousand Minds
greatWorks.summary: A multi-agent discussion surface that treats collaborative reasoning as the product.

highlights: Explores multi-agent UX instead of standard single-thread chat.
Positions AI collaboration as a product and interface problem.
Recent repo activity makes it a visible late-era city on the map.
productionQueue: Expand orchestration controls for participants and roles.
Polish the interaction model for longer collaborative sessions.
Document design lessons learned from multi-agent discussion UX.

architectureNotes: Builds around multi-agent conversation as a first-class interaction model.
Treats clarity of discussion flow as part of the core product value.
